American Culture: Homes in the Suburbs

📖 American Culture Notes

Many American families live in the suburbs. These are quiet neighborhoods outside the big city. Houses usually have a front yard and a backyard for barbecues. People park their cars in the garage.

🗣️ Everyday Dialogue

Agent: This is a beautiful house. It has three bedrooms and two bathrooms.

Buyer: Is there a garage?

Agent: Yes, a two-car garage. And a big backyard!

Buyer: Awesome. I love it.

🎥 Video Lesson: Watch VOA Learning English Lesson 5 on YouTube

Free Study Materials: VOA Learning English • Oxford English Resources

📚 Section 1: Vocabulary Boost

My Home Vocabulary (20 Items)

Category 1: Rooms of the House (10 Items)

🛋️
Living room
The room where you relax and watch TV
"The sofa is in the living room."
🍳
Kitchen
The room where you cook food
"My mother cooks in the kitchen."
🛏️
Bedroom
The room where you sleep
"I have a big bedroom with a comfortable bed."
🚿
Bathroom
The room with a shower and toilet
"The bathroom is upstairs."
🍽️
Dining room
The room where you eat meals
"We eat dinner in the dining room."
🏡
Garden / Yard
The outdoor area around a house
"The children play in the yard."
🚗
Garage
The place where you park your car
"The car is parked in the garage."
📦
Basement
The room below ground level
"We store old boxes in the basement."
🏚️
Attic
The space under the roof
"The attic is full of old paintings."
🚪
Hallway
The passage connecting rooms
"Walk down the hallway to find the bathroom."

Category 2: Furniture & Appliances (10 Items)

🪑
Table & Chairs
Furniture for eating or working
"There is a wooden table and four chairs."
🛋️
Sofa / Couch
A comfortable long seat
"The sofa is very comfortable."
🛏️
Bed
Furniture used for sleeping
"My bed is next to the large window."
👔
Wardrobe / Closet
A tall cabinet for hanging clothes
"My clothes are in the bedroom closet."
🧊
Refrigerator / Fridge
Appliance to keep food cold
"The fresh milk is inside the fridge."
💡
Lamp
A light fixture
"There is a bright reading lamp on the desk."
🖥️
Desk
A table used for study or work
"I do my English homework at my desk."
📚
Bookcase
Shelves for keeping books
"The bookcase is filled with storybooks."
🪞
Mirror
Glass that reflects your image
"She looks in the mirror before going out."
🍲
Stove & Oven
Appliance for cooking meals
"The soup is boiling on the stove."
📝 Section 2: Grammar Focus

There is / There are — Prepositions of Place

There is (Singular): Used with singular nouns. There is a sofa in the living room.
There are (Plural): Used with plural nouns. There are three bedrooms upstairs.
Prepositions of Place: Describe where something is located. The book is on the table. The cat is under the chair.

Grammar Practice Exercises (15 Items)

1. _____ a mirror in the bathroom.

2. _____ four chairs in the dining room.

3. The cat is sleeping _____ the table.

4. _____ any windows in the basement?

5. There _____ a lamp on the study desk.

6. The fridge is _____ the kitchen.

7. There _____ two cars in the garage.

8. Is _____ a television in your bedroom?

9. The sofa is right _____ to the armchair.

10. There _____ any milk in the fridge.

11. The garden is _____ the house.

12. _____ a rug on the living room floor.

13. The books are _____ the bookcase.

14. There _____ five posters on the wall.

15. Is there a garage? Yes, there _____.

🎧 Section 3: Listening Comprehension

Listening: House Tour & Descriptions (15 Items)

Item 1:

How many bedrooms are in the house?

Item 2:

What is in the kitchen?

Item 3:

Where is the cat sleeping?

Item 4:

What does the house have?

Item 5:

Where is the TV located?

Item 6:

How many chairs are in the study room?

Item 7:

Where is the car?

Item 8:

Where is the bedroom?

Item 9:

What is above the bathroom sink?

Item 10:

When does the family eat dinner?

Item 11:

What is stored in the basement?

Item 12:

Where is the lamp?

Item 13:

Why are there no stairs?

Item 14:

What is in the garden?

Item 15:

What color is the sofa?

🗣️ Section 4: Pronunciation & Oral Drills

Oral Drills: Describing Your Home (15 Drills)

1. "There is a sofa in the living room."
💡 Practice 'There is' with singular nouns.
2. "There are two bedrooms upstairs."
💡 Practice 'There are' with plural nouns.
3. "The lamp is on the wooden table."
💡 Focus on preposition 'on'.
4. "The cat is resting under the chair."
💡 Focus on preposition 'under'.
5. "Is there a garage in your house?"
💡 Practice question intonation.
6. "Yes, there is a one-car garage."
💡 Practice short affirmative answers.
7. "No, there isn't a swimming pool."
💡 Practice short negative answers.
8. "Where is the kitchen located?"
💡 Wh-question practice.
9. "The kitchen is next to the dining room."
💡 Focus on 'next to'.
10. "My bedroom is cozy and bright."
💡 Describing personal spaces.
11. "There are books on the bookshelf."
💡 Plural noun drill.
12. "Is there a mirror in the hallway?"
💡 Question practice with 'is there'.
13. "The car is inside the garage."
💡 Preposition 'inside' drill.
14. "There aren't any chairs in the hall."
💡 Negative plural drill 'there aren't'.
15. "We eat breakfast in the kitchen every morning."
💡 Daily home routine phrase.
📖 Section 5: Reading Comprehension

Reading: My New Apartment in Chicago

Hello! My name is Mark. I live in a new apartment in Chicago. My apartment is on the third floor. It has a spacious living room, a modern kitchen, one bedroom, and a small bathroom. In the living room, there is a gray sofa, a coffee table, and a large TV on the wall. My bedroom has a comfortable bed and a big closet for my clothes. I don't have a garden, but there is a lovely balcony with green plants. I really love my home!

Comprehension Questions (15 Items)

Question 1: Where does Mark live?

Question 2: What floor is his apartment on?

Question 3: How many bedrooms are in his apartment?

Question 4: What color is the sofa in the living room?

Question 5: Where is the large TV?

Question 6: Does Mark have a garden?

Question 7: What is on the balcony?

Question 8: What is in his bedroom?

Question 9: Is the kitchen modern or old?

Question 10: How does Mark feel about his home?

Question 11: Is the apartment on the ground floor?

Question 12: Where is the closet located?

Question 13: What is next to the sofa?

Question 14: How many bathrooms are there?

Question 15: What city is the apartment in?

✏️ Section 6: Grammar & Writing Quiz

Writing & Sentence Structure: Home Descriptions (15 Items)

1. Complete: There _____ a rug on the floor.

2. Complete: There _____ three pillows on the couch.

3. Fill in: The keys are _____ top of the kitchen table.

4. Fill in: My desk is placed _____ the window and the bed.

5. Choose correct form: _____ there a TV in your room?

6. Choose correct form: _____ there any towels in the bathroom?

7. Fill in: The shoes are _____ the bed.

8. Complete: There _____ a garage behind the house.

9. Complete: There _____ many trees in the yard.

10. Fill in: We store old boxes _____ the basement.

11. Choose: Is there a garden? No, there _____.

12. Choose: Are there two bathrooms? Yes, there _____.

13. Fill in: The clock is hanging _____ the wall.

14. Complete: There isn't _____ armchair in the bedroom.

15. Complete: There aren't _____ books on the table.

🏆 Section 7: Vocabulary & Grammar Check

Unit 5 Review: My Home & Furniture (15 Items)

Question 1: Which room is used for sleeping?

💡 Note: Bedroom is the room with a bed.

Question 2: Where do you park a car?

💡 Note: Garage is used for parking vehicles.

Question 3: Complete: _____ a sofa in the living room.

💡 Note: 'Sofa' is singular so use 'There is'.

Question 4: Complete: _____ three chairs around the table.

💡 Note: 'Three chairs' is plural so use 'There are'.

Question 5: Where is the milk kept?

💡 Note: Fridge keeps food cold.

Question 6: Where do you cook meals?

💡 Note: Kitchen is for cooking.

Question 7: The cat is hiding _____ the bed.

💡 Note: Under means beneath.

Question 8: Is there a bathroom on the first floor? Yes, _____.

💡 Note: Affirmative answer for singular is 'there is'.

Question 9: Are there any plants in the garden? Yes, _____.

💡 Note: Affirmative answer for plural is 'there are'.

Question 10: Where do you hang your clothes?

💡 Note: Closet / wardrobe stores clothes.

Question 11: Complete: The lamp is _____ the desk.

💡 Note: 'On' indicates surface contact.

Question 12: Which space is under the roof?

💡 Note: The attic is under the roof.

Question 13: Which space is below ground level?

💡 Note: The basement is below ground.

Question 14: Complete: There _____ any pictures on the wall.

💡 Note: 'Pictures' is plural negative so 'aren't'.

Question 15: Complete: The dining table is _____ the living room and kitchen.

💡 Note: 'Between' refers to a middle position.

American Culture: Around Town & Directions

📖 American Culture Notes

American cities often use a grid pattern with numbered streets and avenues. A block is the space between two intersecting streets. People ask for directions using polite phrases like 'Excuse me, where is...?' or 'How do I get to...?'. Downtown is the commercial center of a city.

🗣️ Everyday Dialogue

Tourist: Excuse me! Is there a bank near here?

Local: Yes, there is one on Main Street. Go straight for two blocks and turn left.

Tourist: Is it next to the supermarket?

Local: That's right! It's right opposite the city park.

🎥 Video Lesson: Watch VOA Learning English Lesson 6 on YouTube

Free Study Materials: VOA Learning English • Oxford English Resources

📚 Section 1: Vocabulary Boost

Around Town Vocabulary (20 Items)

Category 1: Places in Town (10 Items)

🏦
Bank
A place to keep or withdraw money
"I need to deposit money at the bank."
📮
Post Office
A place to buy stamps and mail letters
"Send this package at the post office."
🛒
Supermarket
A large grocery store
"We buy fresh vegetables at the supermarket."
📚
Library
A place to borrow books and study
"The public library is very quiet."
🌳
Park
A green outdoor public area
"Children play soccer in the city park."
🏥
Hospital
A medical center for sick people
"The ambulance drove to the hospital."
💊
Pharmacy / Drugstore
A store selling medicines
"Buy your aspirin at the pharmacy."
🚌
Bus Station
Where buses arrive and depart
"Wait for bus line 5 at the bus station."
🍽️
Restaurant
A place where cooked meals are served
"We ate dinner at an Italian restaurant."
🎬
Movie Theater / Cinema
A venue for watching films
"Let's watch a film at the movie theater."

Category 2: Directions & Location Phrases (10 Items)

⬅️
Turn left
Change direction to the left
"Turn left at the next traffic light."
➡️
Turn right
Change direction to the right
"Turn right onto 5th Avenue."
⬆️
Go straight ahead
Move forward without turning
"Go straight ahead for two blocks."
🚶
Cross the street
Walk from one side of the road to the other
"Cross the street carefully."
📐
On the corner
Where two streets meet
"The coffee shop is on the corner."
↔️
Opposite / Across from
Facing something on the other side
"The library is opposite the park."
👥
Next to
Beside or by the side of
"The bakery is next to the pharmacy."
⏸️
Between
In the middle of two places
"The bank is between the museum and cafe."
🚦
Traffic light
Signal light controlling street traffic
"Stop when the traffic light turns red."
🏙️
Block
Distance from one street intersection to the next
"Walk three blocks north."
📝 Section 2: Grammar Focus

Directions, Imperatives & Prepositions of Location

Imperatives for Directions: Use base verbs to give directions. Turn right. Go straight. Cross the street.
Prepositions of Location: Describe spatial position in town. The bank is opposite the park. The hospital is next to the library.
Asking for Directions: Polite questions for finding places. Excuse me, where is the post office? How do I get to the bus station?

Grammar Practice Exercises (15 Items)

1. _____ left at the intersection.

2. Go _____ ahead for two blocks.

3. The supermarket is _____ to the post office.

4. Excuse me, _____ is the nearest bank?

5. _____ the street at the pedestrian crossing.

6. The library is _____ the park and the school.

7. Turn right _____ 3rd Avenue.

8. The pharmacy is on the _____ of Elm Street.

9. _____ straight until you see the gas station.

10. How do I _____ to the train station?

11. The coffee shop is _____ from the bank.

12. Don't _____ left here; it's a one-way street.

13. The hospital is _____ Second Street.

14. Is there a post office near here? Yes, there _____ one nearby.

15. _____ me, can you help me find the hotel?

🎧 Section 3: Listening Comprehension

Listening: Asking & Giving Directions (15 Items)

Item 1:

Where is the public library?

Item 2:

What should you do after two blocks?

Item 3:

What is next to the pharmacy?

Item 4:

Where is the bank after turning right?

Item 5:

How far is the bus station?

Item 6:

What is across from the hospital?

Item 7:

Where is the Italian restaurant?

Item 8:

What direction should the person follow?

Item 9:

What is next to the museum?

Item 10:

Where is the post office?

Item 11:

When should you cross the street?

Item 12:

What street is the movie theater on?

Item 13:

What building do you walk past?

Item 14:

What direction do you walk?

Item 15:

Where is the train station?

🗣️ Section 4: Pronunciation & Oral Drills

Oral Drills: Navigation & Directions (15 Drills)

1. "Excuse me, where is the nearest bank?"
💡 Practice polite question intonation.
2. "Go straight ahead for two blocks."
💡 Practice imperative direction phrase.
3. "Turn left at the next intersection."
💡 Practice direction command 'Turn left'.
4. "Turn right onto Main Street."
💡 Practice direction command 'Turn right'.
5. "The post office is next to the pharmacy."
💡 Practice preposition 'next to'.
6. "The library is opposite the park."
💡 Practice preposition 'opposite'.
7. "The cafe is on the corner of 4th Street."
💡 Practice 'on the corner of'.
8. "Cross the street carefully at the crosswalk."
💡 Safety command drill.
9. "How do I get to the city hospital?"
💡 Asking 'How do I get to...?'
10. "Walk past the supermarket."
💡 Verb phrase 'Walk past'.
11. "It is between the bakery and the bank."
💡 Practice 'between'.
12. "Is there a restaurant near here?"
💡 Asking 'Is there a... near here?'
13. "Yes, there is one across from the hotel."
💡 Answering location questions.
14. "Go down this street and turn left."
💡 Direction sentence drill.
15. "Thank you for your help! You're welcome!"
💡 Polite gratitude exchange.
📖 Section 5: Reading Comprehension

Reading: Exploring Downtown San Diego

Welcome to San Diego! Downtown San Diego is easy to explore on foot. If you start at the Central Station, walk straight ahead on Broadway for three blocks. On your right, you will see the City Park. Opposite the park, there is a large public library. If you need money, there is a bank on the corner of Broadway and 4th Avenue, right next to a famous seafood restaurant. To reach the harbor, cross Pacific Highway at the traffic light. Enjoy your day around town!

Comprehension Questions (15 Items)

Question 1: Which city is this reading passage about?

Question 2: Is downtown San Diego easy to explore on foot?

Question 3: Where do you start walking from?

Question 4: How many blocks do you walk on Broadway?

Question 5: Where is the City Park located?

Question 6: What is opposite the park?

Question 7: Where is the bank located?

Question 8: What is right next to the bank?

Question 9: What street do you cross to reach the harbor?

Question 10: Where should you cross Pacific Highway?

Question 11: What kind of restaurant is next to the bank?

Question 12: What building is opposite the City Park?

Question 13: Is there a bank in downtown San Diego?

Question 14: What street do you walk on from Central Station?

Question 15: What is the general tone of the passage?

✏️ Section 6: Grammar & Writing Quiz

Writing & Sentence Structure: City Directions (15 Items)

1. Complete: _____ left at the corner of 5th street.

2. Fill in: Walk straight _____ for three blocks.

3. Fill in: The bank is _____ the supermarket.

4. Complete: Excuse me, _____ is the bus stop?

5. Fill in: The library is _____ the park and the hospital.

6. Complete: _____ the street at the pedestrian zebra line.

7. Fill in: The restaurant is opposite _____ the hotel.

8. Complete: _____ right onto Broadway Avenue.

9. Fill in: There is a pharmacy _____ the corner.

10. Complete: How do I _____ to the train station?

11. Fill in: Go past _____ bank and turn right.

12. Complete: Don't _____ straight; turn right here.

13. Fill in: The park is right _____ the library.

14. Complete: _____ me, is there a post office near here?

15. Fill in: Stop when the traffic light is _____.

🏆 Section 7: Vocabulary & Grammar Check

Unit 6 Review: Around Town & Directions (15 Items)

Question 1: Where do you borrow books?

💡 Note: Library is for borrowing books.

Question 2: Where do you buy medicines?

💡 Note: Pharmacy sells medical supplies.

Question 3: Which direction phrase means moving forward without turning?

💡 Note: Go straight means keep moving forward.

Question 4: Complete: Turn _____ at the traffic light.

💡 Note: Turn left or right are valid directions.

Question 5: Complete: The bank is _____ to the supermarket.

💡 Note: 'Next to' means beside.

Question 6: Where do you mail letters and packages?

💡 Note: Post office handles mail.

Question 7: Complete: Excuse me, _____ do I get to the hospital?

💡 Note: 'How do I get to...?' asks for directions.

Question 8: What signal controls street traffic?

💡 Note: Traffic light manages traffic flow.

Question 9: Complete: The coffee shop is _____ the corner.

💡 Note: We say 'on the corner'.

Question 10: Which phrase means walking from one side of the street to the other?

💡 Note: Cross means move across.

Question 11: Where do you go to see a film?

💡 Note: Movie theater shows films.

Question 12: Complete: The museum is _____ the park.

💡 Note: Opposite means facing directly across.

Question 13: Where do buses arrive and depart?

💡 Note: Bus station is for bus transit.

Question 14: Complete: Walk two _____ down Broadway.

💡 Note: Blocks measure city distance.

Question 15: Complete: The school is _____ the library and the park.

💡 Note: Between links two distinct reference points.

American Culture: Clothes and Shopping

📖 American Culture Notes

American shopping malls and outlet centers are popular places to shop. People wear casual clothes like jeans, T-shirts, and sneakers every day. When shopping, salespeople often ask 'Can I help you find something?' or 'What size are you looking for?'.

🗣️ Everyday Dialogue

Clerk: Hi! Can I help you find anything today?

Customer: Yes, please. How much is this blue jacket?

Clerk: It's on sale for $45. What size do you need?

Customer: Medium, please. Can I try it on in the fitting room?

Clerk: Sure! The fitting room is right over there.

🎥 Video Lesson: Watch VOA Learning English Lesson 7 on YouTube

Free Study Materials: VOA Learning English • Oxford English Resources

📚 Section 1: Vocabulary Boost

Clothes & Shopping Vocabulary (20 Items)

Category 1: Clothing & Footwear (10 Items)

👕
T-shirt
A casual short-sleeved cotton shirt
"He is wearing a white T-shirt."
👔
Shirt
A formal button-up garment
"I wear a blue shirt to work."
👖
Pants / Jeans
Trousers worn on the lower body
"These denim jeans are very comfortable."
👗
Dress
A one-piece garment for women
"She bought an elegant red dress."
🥻
Skirt
A garment hanging from the waist
"She is wearing a black skirt."
🧥
Jacket / Coat
Outer clothing worn for warmth
"Put on your warm coat; it is cold outside."
👞
Shoes
Footwear worn outdoors
"He polished his black leather shoes."
👟
Sneakers
Casual athletic shoes
"I wear white sneakers for walking."
🧢
Hat / Cap
A covering for the head
"He wears a baseball cap in the sun."
🧦
Socks
Garment worn on the feet inside shoes
"I need a pair of warm winter socks."

Category 2: Shopping & Money Terms (10 Items)

🏷️
Price tag
Label showing how much an item costs
"Check the price tag before buying."
🚪
Fitting room
Private room to try on clothes
"Where is the fitting room?"
🧑‍💼
Cashier
Person who collects payment in a store
"Pay for your clothes at the cashier."
📐
Size (S / M / L / XL)
Dimensions of clothing items
"Do you have this sweater in size Large?"
💰
Cheap
Low in price or cost
"This shirt is very cheap; it's only $10."
💎
Expensive
High in price or costing a lot of money
"That designer coat is too expensive."
🏷️
Discount / Sale
Reduction in regular price
"There is a 30% discount on all shoes."
🧾
Receipt
Paper showing proof of payment
"Keep your receipt for returns."
💳
Credit card
Plastic card used to pay electronically
"Can I pay with a credit card?"
💵
Cash
Paper money and coins
"I paid $20 in cash."
📝 Section 2: Grammar Focus

Present Continuous for Wearing — Demonstratives & Prices

Present Continuous for Wearing: Use am/is/are + wearing for current clothing. She is wearing a red jacket today. They are wearing sneakers.
Demonstratives (This/That/These/Those): This/These (near), That/Those (far). This shirt (singular near). Those shoes (plural far).
Asking Prices (How much): How much is (singular) / How much are (plural). How much is this sweater? How much are these jeans?

Grammar Practice Exercises (15 Items)

1. Look! She _____ a beautiful yellow dress.

2. How much _____ this pair of shoes?

3. How much _____ those blue pants?

4. I like _____ shirt right here in my hand.

5. Look at _____ coat over there on the wall.

6. Are _____ your sneakers near the door?

7. Look at _____ high boots over there!

8. He _____ a black jacket today.

9. They _____ casual clothes to the park.

10. How much _____ that digital watch?

11. _____ shoes are too small for me.

12. Excuse me, how much _____ these sunglasses?

13. What color is _____ hat you are holding?

14. She _____ wearing a winter coat because it's warm.

15. Can I try on _____ pants over there?

🎧 Section 3: Listening Comprehension

Listening: In the Clothes Store (15 Items)

Item 1:

How much is the red coat?

Item 2:

What is Sarah wearing?

Item 3:

What size does the customer want?

Item 4:

Is the jacket on sale?

Item 5:

Where is the fitting room?

Item 6:

How much are the three shirts?

Item 7:

How is the customer paying?

Item 8:

How much do the sneakers cost?

Item 9:

What looks expensive?

Item 10:

What color is David's shirt?

Item 11:

What does the clerk hand the customer?

Item 12:

How much is the yellow hat?

Item 13:

What size coat is needed?

Item 14:

Is the fitting room free?

Item 15:

How many pairs of socks did she buy?

🗣️ Section 4: Pronunciation & Oral Drills

Oral Drills: Clothes & Shopping (15 Drills)

1. "How much is this blue jacket?"
💡 Asking price for singular item.
2. "How much are these leather shoes?"
💡 Asking price for plural item.
3. "She is wearing a beautiful red dress."
💡 Present Continuous clothing drill.
4. "He is wearing jeans and a white T-shirt."
💡 Describing everyday outfits.
5. "Can I try this sweater on in the fitting room?"
💡 Asking permission to try clothes.
6. "Do you have this shirt in size Large?"
💡 Inquiring about clothing size.
7. "This hat is very cheap, only ten dollars."
💡 Using adjective 'cheap'.
8. "That leather coat is too expensive for me."
💡 Using adjective 'expensive'.
9. "I would like to pay by credit card."
💡 Stating payment method.
10. "Keep your receipt for any clothing returns."
💡 Vocabulary practice 'receipt'.
11. "Look at those boots over there on the shelf."
💡 Demonstrative 'those' drill.
12. "I love this soft scarf around my neck."
💡 Demonstrative 'this' drill.
13. "What size are you looking for today?"
💡 Clerk question drill.
14. "There is a big discount on winter clothes."
💡 Shopping phrase drill.
15. "Thank you for shopping with us today!"
💡 Customer service farewell.
📖 Section 5: Reading Comprehension

Reading: Shopping at the Mall

Saturday is shopping day for Maria and her sister Anna. Today, they are visiting the Grand City Mall. The mall is very busy. Maria is looking for a new outfit for her friend's birthday party. She finds a pink dress that costs $40. She goes to the fitting room to try it on. It fits perfectly! Anna wants a pair of comfortable shoes. She sees white sneakers on sale for $30. They buy the clothes, pay the cashier with a credit card, and get their receipts. They are very happy with their new purchases!

Comprehension Questions (15 Items)

Question 1: What day do Maria and Anna go shopping?

Question 2: Which mall are they visiting?

Question 3: Why is Maria looking for a new outfit?

Question 4: What color dress does Maria find?

Question 5: How much does the dress cost?

Question 6: Where does Maria try on the dress?

Question 7: Does the dress fit Maria?

Question 8: What does Anna want to buy?

Question 9: What type of shoes does Anna see?

Question 10: How much do the sneakers cost?

Question 11: How do they pay the cashier?

Question 12: What do they receive after paying?

Question 13: How do they feel at the end?

Question 14: Is the mall quiet or busy?

Question 15: Who goes shopping with Maria?

✏️ Section 6: Grammar & Writing Quiz

Writing & Sentence Structure: Shopping Expressions (15 Items)

1. Complete: She _____ wearing a green coat today.

2. Fill in: How much _____ these denim jeans?

3. Fill in: How much _____ that red hat?

4. Complete: Look at _____ shoes in my hand.

5. Fill in: Can I try this shirt on in the _____ room?

6. Complete: He is _____ black sneakers right now.

7. Fill in: I would like to pay in _____.

8. Complete: That coat is $200! It is very _____.

9. Fill in: This T-shirt is only $5! It is very _____.

10. Complete: What _____ are you? I am a Size Medium.

11. Fill in: Don't forget to get your _____ from the cashier.

12. Complete: Look at _____ hat on the high shelf over there.

13. Fill in: _____ are my favorite pair of socks.

14. Complete: They _____ wearing winter jackets.

15. Fill in: There is a 50 percent _____ on summer dresses.

🏆 Section 7: Vocabulary & Grammar Check

Unit 7 Review: Clothes & Shopping (15 Items)

Question 1: What garment do you wear on your feet inside shoes?

💡 Note: Socks are worn on feet.

Question 2: Complete: She _____ a pretty red dress right now.

💡 Note: Present continuous for present action.

Question 3: How do you ask the price of plural items like jeans?

💡 Note: 'Jeans' is plural so use 'are'.

Question 4: Where do you try on clothing in a store?

💡 Note: Fitting room is for trying on clothes.

Question 5: What plastic card is used for electronic payment?

💡 Note: Credit card pays electronically.

Question 6: Opposite of 'expensive' is:

💡 Note: Cheap means low in price.

Question 7: Complete: Look at _____ sweater right here.

💡 Note: Singular item near speaker is 'this'.

Question 8: Complete: Look at _____ shoes over there across the room.

💡 Note: Plural items far from speaker are 'those'.

Question 9: What paper proves you paid for an item?

💡 Note: Receipt is proof of payment.

Question 10: Complete: What size do you need? I need size _____.

💡 Note: Medium is a standard clothing size.

Question 11: Complete: They _____ wearing raincoats because of the storm.

💡 Note: 'They' takes 'are' in continuous tense.

Question 12: What label shows how much something costs?

💡 Note: Price tag shows cost.

Question 13: Complete: How much _____ that gold watch?

💡 Note: Gold watch is singular so use 'is'.

Question 14: Which footwear is typically worn for sports?

💡 Note: Sneakers are athletic shoes.

Question 15: Complete: He is wearing a blue shirt and black _____.

💡 Note: Pants complete the outfit.

American Culture: Free Time and Hobbies

📖 American Culture Notes

Americans enjoy spending their free time outdoors or doing sports like baseball, basketball, and running. On weekends, people often meet friends for brunch, work on gardening projects, or relax with hobbies like cooking and photography.

🗣️ Everyday Dialogue

Alex: What do you usually do on weekends, Sam?

Sam: I usually play soccer on Saturday morning and read books on Sunday.

Alex: Nice! Do you like watching movies?

Sam: Yes! I love watching action movies with my family.

🎥 Video Lesson: Watch VOA Learning English Lesson 8 on YouTube

Free Study Materials: VOA Learning English • Oxford English Resources

📚 Section 1: Vocabulary Boost

Free Time & Hobbies Vocabulary (20 Items)

Category 1: Hobbies & Activities (10 Items)

📚
Reading books
Looking at and understanding written words
"I enjoy reading novels before sleeping."
⚽
Playing soccer
Kicking a ball to score goals in teams
"He plays soccer every Saturday."
🎧
Listening to music
Enjoying audio songs and tunes
"She loves listening to jazz music."
🍳
Cooking dinner
Preparing food for meals
"My father likes cooking dinner."
🎬
Watching movies
Viewing films at home or cinema
"We watch movies on Friday nights."
🏊
Swimming
Moving through water using arms and legs
"Swimming is great exercise."
🏃
Running / Jogging
Running at a steady pace for fitness
"He goes running in the park."
🪴
Gardening
Growing flowers and plants
"My grandmother enjoys gardening."
🎸
Playing guitar
Making music with a string instrument
"He is learning to play the guitar."
✈️
Traveling
Visiting new cities or countries
"They love traveling in the summer."

Category 2: Frequency & Time Words (10 Items)

💯
Always (100%)
At all times; without exception
"I always drink coffee in the morning."
📊
Usually (80%)
Under normal conditions; generally
"She usually walks to work."
🌊
Often (60%)
Frequently; many times
"We often go to the beach."
⚖️
Sometimes (50%)
Occasionally; on some occasions
"He sometimes eats fast food."
⏳
Hardly ever (10%)
Almost never; rarely
"They hardly ever watch TV."
🚫
Never (0%)
Not at any time; not ever
"I never drink soda."
📅
Every day
Daily; each day of the week
"She practices piano every day."
🗓️
On weekends
On Saturday and Sunday
"What do you do on weekends?"
1️⃣
Once a week
One time during a 7-day period
"I play tennis once a week."
🌆
In the evening
During the late afternoon/night hours
"We relax in the evening."
📝 Section 2: Grammar Focus

Adverbs of Frequency & Likes / Dislikes (-ing)

Adverbs of Frequency Position: Place adverbs before main verbs, but AFTER the verb to be. I always read books. She is usually happy.
Verbs of Liking + -ing: After like, love, enjoy, hate, use verb+ing. I love playing soccer. She enjoys cooking dinner.
Questions about Routine: How often do you...? / What do you do on...? How often do you go swimming? What do you do on weekends?

Grammar Practice Exercises (15 Items)

1. I _____ drink coffee in the morning. (100%)

2. She enjoys _____ to music in her bedroom.

3. They _____ play soccer on Sundays. (80%)

4. How _____ do you go to the gym?

5. He loves _____ books about history.

6. We _____ eat fast food. (0%)

7. She is _____ late for school.

8. Do you like _____ in the ocean?

9. They hate _____ in the rain.

10. I play tennis _____ a week.

11. What do you do _____ weekends?

12. She _____ goes to bed before 10 PM. (60%)

13. My father enjoys _____ dinner for the family.

14. How often _____ you watch movies?

15. He _____ ever plays video games.

🎧 Section 3: Listening Comprehension

Listening: Hobbies & Free Time (15 Items)

Item 1:

What does Mark do every morning?

Item 2:

What activity does the speaker prefer?

Item 3:

How often does Emily travel?

Item 4:

What does the sister love doing on weekends?

Item 5:

When does the speaker listen to classical music?

Item 6:

How often does John watch TV?

Item 7:

What is the favorite activity?

Item 8:

What instrument is she learning?

Item 9:

When do they play soccer?

Item 10:

What genre of books does the speaker love?

Item 11:

How often is the room cleaned?

Item 12:

What day do they watch movies?

Item 13:

Does he like running in cold weather?

Item 14:

Where does she swim?

Item 15:

What time do they eat breakfast?

🗣️ Section 4: Pronunciation & Oral Drills

Oral Drills: Talking About Free Time (15 Drills)

1. "I always drink coffee in the morning."
💡 Adverb of frequency 'always' drill.
2. "She usually reads books on weekends."
💡 Adverb of frequency 'usually' drill.
3. "Do you like listening to pop music?"
💡 Question format with 'like + -ing'.
4. "Yes, I love listening to music!"
💡 Affirmative answer with 'love + -ing'.
5. "How often do you go to the gym?"
💡 Wh-question 'How often' drill.
6. "I go swimming twice a week."
💡 Expressing frequency 'twice a week'.
7. "My father enjoys cooking dinner."
💡 Practice 'enjoys + -ing'.
8. "They hate waiting in long lines."
💡 Practice 'hate + -ing'.
9. "He hardly ever watches television."
💡 Practice 'hardly ever'.
10. "We sometimes eat out at restaurants."
💡 Practice 'sometimes'.
11. "What are your favorite hobbies?"
💡 General hobby inquiry.
12. "I love traveling to new places."
💡 Expressing passion for travel.
13. "She plays the guitar very well."
💡 Describing musical skills.
14. "Do you go running every morning?"
💡 Routine inquiry drill.
15. "Never say never when trying new hobbies!"
💡 Fun idiomatic drill.
📖 Section 5: Reading Comprehension

Reading: David's Busy Weekend Routine

David is a high school teacher in Boston. He works hard during the week, so he loves his weekends! On Saturday morning, David always wakes up early at 7:00 AM and goes running along the Charles River. After running, he usually meets his friend Lisa at a local cafe for breakfast. In the afternoon, he enjoys working in his garden or reading mystery novels. On Sunday evenings, David and his family always watch a movie together and cook a delicious homemade dinner. He never works on Sundays because family time is important to him.

Comprehension Questions (15 Items)

Question 1: What is David's profession?

Question 2: In which city does David live?

Question 3: What time does he wake up on Saturday morning?

Question 4: Where does he go running?

Question 5: Who does he meet at the cafe for breakfast?

Question 6: What does he enjoy doing on Saturday afternoon?

Question 7: What does David's family do on Sunday evenings?

Question 8: Does David work on Sundays?

Question 9: Why doesn't he work on Sundays?

Question 10: How often does he wake up early on Saturday?

Question 11: What genre of books does David read?

Question 12: Does David run before or after breakfast?

Question 13: What meal does David eat with Lisa?

Question 14: Does David like his weekends?

Question 15: What does David cook on Sunday evening?

✏️ Section 6: Grammar & Writing Quiz

Writing & Sentence Structure: Routine & Hobbies (15 Items)

1. Complete: I _____ (100%) eat breakfast at 8 AM.

2. Fill in: She loves _____ to classical music.

3. Complete: They _____ (0%) arrive late for class.

4. Fill in: How _____ do you practice the piano?

5. Complete: He enjoys _____ soccer with friends.

6. Fill in: We go swimming _____ a week on Saturdays.

7. Complete: My mother _____ (80%) cooks dinner.

8. Fill in: Do you like _____ books in English?

9. Complete: They hate _____ up early on Sundays.

10. Fill in: I go jogging _____ the morning.

11. Complete: She is _____ (50%) happy on Mondays.

12. Fill in: What do you do _____ weekends?

13. Complete: We _____ (60%) watch movies on Friday.

14. Fill in: He enjoys _____ photos of nature.

15. Complete: They _____ ever go to bed after midnight.

🏆 Section 7: Vocabulary & Grammar Check

Unit 8 Review: Free Time & Hobbies (15 Items)

Question 1: Which adverb of frequency means 100% of the time?

💡 Note: 'Always' means 100% frequency.

Question 2: Complete: She loves _____ tennis on weekends.

💡 Note: Use verb-ing after 'loves'.

Question 3: Which adverb means 0% of the time?

💡 Note: 'Never' means 0% frequency.

Question 4: Complete: How _____ do you go running?

💡 Note: 'How often' asks about frequency.

Question 5: Where does the adverb of frequency go in a simple sentence?

💡 Note: Adverbs go before main verbs.

Question 6: Complete: I enjoy _____ meals for my family.

💡 Note: Use verb-ing after 'enjoy'.

Question 7: What hobby involves kicking a ball into a goal?

💡 Note: Soccer involves kicking a ball.

Question 8: Complete: We watch movies _____ Friday nights.

💡 Note: We say 'on Friday nights'.

Question 9: Complete: They _____ (80%) walk to school.

💡 Note: 'Usually' represents ~80% frequency.

Question 10: What hobby involves growing flowers and plants?

💡 Note: Gardening is growing plants.

Question 11: Complete: He hates _____ in long traffic jams.

💡 Note: Use verb-ing after 'hates'.

Question 12: How do you say 'one time in a week'?

💡 Note: 'Once a week' means 1 time per week.

Question 13: Complete: She is _____ cheerful in the morning.

💡 Note: Adverb goes AFTER the verb to be.

Question 14: What hobby involves playing an instrument with strings?

💡 Note: Guitar is a stringed instrument.

Question 15: Complete: I hardly _____ watch horror movies.

💡 Note: 'Hardly ever' means almost never.

American Culture: Abilities and Talents

📖 American Culture Notes

In American schools and workplaces, people encourage sharing unique talents. School talent shows, community sports leagues, and music clubs give people a chance to show what they can do well.

🗣️ Everyday Dialogue

Emma: Can you play any musical instruments, Mike?

Mike: Yes, I can play the piano, but I can't play the guitar.

Emma: That's cool! Can you speak Spanish?

Mike: I can speak a little bit, but not fluently.

🎥 Video Lesson: Watch VOA Learning English Lesson 9 on YouTube

Free Study Materials: VOA Learning English • Oxford English Resources

📚 Section 1: Vocabulary Boost

Abilities & Talents Vocabulary (20 Items)

Category 1: Skills & Action Verbs (10 Items)

🗣️
Speak English
Communicate verbally using the English language
"She can speak English fluently."
🏊
Swim
Move through water using body motions
"He can swim across the pool."
🚗
Drive a car
Operate a motor vehicle safely
"Can you drive a manual car?"
🍝
Cook Italian food
Prepare delicious meals from Italy
"My mom can cook delicious lasagna."
🎹
Play piano
Perform music on a keyboard instrument
"She plays the piano beautifully."
💃
Dance salsa
Move rhythmically to Latin salsa beats
"They can dance salsa very well."
🎤
Sing songs
Produce musical sounds with your voice
"He can sing opera songs."
🎨
Paint pictures
Create artistic images with brush and paint
"She can paint landscape pictures."
🚲
Ride a bicycle
Balance and pedal a two-wheeled bike
"The child learned to ride a bicycle."
💻
Fix computers
Repair software and hardware technical issues
"He can fix any computer problem."

Category 2: Adverbs of Manner & Modifiers (10 Items)

⭐
Very well
With high skill or excellence
"She plays tennis very well."
⚡
Fast / Quickly
At high speed; rapidly
"He can run very fast."
💬
Fluently
Spoken smoothly and accurately without hesitation
"She speaks French fluently."
🧩
Easily
Without difficulty or hard effort
"He solved the math puzzle easily."
🐢
Slowly
At a low speed or pace
"Please speak more slowly."
🤏
A little bit
A small amount or degree
"I can speak German a little bit."
👍
Quite well
Fairly well; to a good degree
"She sings quite well."
🛡️
Carefully
With attention and caution
"Drive carefully on rainy roads."
🔊
Loudly
With great volume or sound
"Don't talk loudly in the library."
🤫
Quietly
With little or no sound
"The students read quietly."
📝 Section 2: Grammar Focus

Modal Verb Can / Can't & Adverbs of Manner

Modal Verb Can / Can't: Use CAN for ability (no 's' for 3rd person, base verb after). I can swim. She can speak English. They can't drive.
Questions with Can: Put CAN before the subject for questions. Can you play piano? Yes, I can. / No, I can't.
Adverbs of Manner: Adverbs describe HOW an action is done. She sings beautifully. He runs fast. They speak fluently.

Grammar Practice Exercises (15 Items)

1. She _____ speak English fluently.

2. _____ you drive a manual car?

3. He _____ swim because he is afraid of water.

4. They can _____ the guitar very well.

5. Can she cook Italian food? Yes, she _____.

6. Can they dance salsa? No, they _____.

7. He runs very _____.

8. She speaks French _____.

9. Please drive _____ on ice.

10. Can you fix this computer? Yes, I _____ do it easily.

11. My brother _____ play the violin.

12. They can't _____ in the deep lake.

13. Speak _____; the baby is sleeping.

14. Can your mother bake cakes? Yes, she _____.

15. I can speak English a _____ bit.

🎧 Section 3: Listening Comprehension

Listening: Talent Survey (15 Items)

Item 1:

What sport can Mark play well?

Item 2:

How many languages can she speak fluently?

Item 3:

Can the brother drive a car?

Item 4:

What type of meals can he cook?

Item 5:

Can the speaker swim far?

Item 6:

How does she play the piano?

Item 7:

Can they fix laptops?

Item 8:

Why does the listener ask the speaker to talk slowly?

Item 9:

What can the sister draw?

Item 10:

How does he drive his motorcycle?

Item 11:

What style of songs can he sing?

Item 12:

Can children dance salsa with practice?

Item 13:

Why can't she play the drums?

Item 14:

Can the person code in Python?

Item 15:

How far can they run?

🗣️ Section 4: Pronunciation & Oral Drills

Oral Drills: Expressing Abilities (15 Drills)

1. "I can speak English fluently."
💡 Expressing affirmative ability.
2. "She can play the piano very well."
💡 Expressing 3rd person ability with 'can'.
3. "He can't drive a manual car."
💡 Expressing negative ability 'can't'.
4. "Can you swim across the pool?"
💡 Practice question intonation with 'can'.
5. "Yes, I can swim very well!"
💡 Affirmative short answer drill.
6. "No, I can't swim at all."
💡 Negative short answer drill.
7. "Please speak more slowly and clearly."
💡 Asking someone to adjust speaking manner.
8. "Drive carefully on the highway."
💡 Adverb of manner 'carefully' drill.
9. "She runs very fast in races."
💡 Adverb of manner 'fast' drill.
10. "They can dance salsa gracefully."
💡 Adverb of manner 'gracefully' drill.
11. "Can he fix this computer problem?"
💡 Ability question drill for 3rd person.
12. "Yes, he can fix it easily."
💡 Expressing effortless skill.
13. "I can speak a little bit of French."
💡 Modifying ability level 'a little bit'.
14. "Can you sing opera songs?"
💡 Musical ability inquiry.
15. "No, I can't sing, but I can play guitar!"
💡 Contrasting abilities drill.
📖 Section 5: Reading Comprehension

Reading: The Talent Show at Lincoln High

Lincoln High School hosts an annual talent show every spring. Students show their amazing talents to teachers and parents. This year, Jessica sang a famous pop song beautifully, and everyone clapped loudly. A student named Kevin played the violin fluently without looking at any sheet music. Another student, Leo, showed that he can solve a Rubik's Cube in less than thirty seconds! However, not everyone can perform on stage. Sam tried to juggle fire torches, but he couldn't do it safely, so he performed magic tricks instead. The talent show was a great success!

Comprehension Questions (15 Items)

Question 1: What event does Lincoln High School host every spring?

Question 2: Who watches the talent show?

Question 3: What did Jessica do on stage?

Question 4: How did Jessica sing?

Question 5: What instrument did Kevin play?

Question 6: Did Kevin look at sheet music while playing?

Question 7: What can Leo solve in less than thirty seconds?

Question 8: What did Sam try to juggle initially?

Question 9: Could Sam juggle fire torches safely?

Question 10: What did Sam perform instead of juggling fire?

Question 11: How did the audience react to Jessica's song?

Question 12: Is the talent show held every spring?

Question 13: Can Kevin play the violin?

Question 14: Was the talent show successful?

Question 15: How fast can Leo solve the Rubik's Cube?

✏️ Section 6: Grammar & Writing Quiz

Writing & Sentence Structure: Abilities & Adverbs (15 Items)

1. Complete: She _____ play the piano very well.

2. Fill in: _____ you speak English?

3. Complete: He _____ drive a car because he has no license.

4. Fill in: They can _____ salsa easily.

5. Complete: Can she cook? Yes, she _____.

6. Fill in: He runs very _____.

7. Complete: Please speak _____ in the library.

8. Fill in: Drive _____ when it rains.

9. Complete: She speaks Spanish _____.

10. Fill in: I can swim _____ the pool.

11. Complete: Can you fix computers? Yes, I _____.

12. Fill in: He can't _____ high in the air.

13. Complete: She sings _____ in the choir.

14. Fill in: They can speak a _____ English.

15. Complete: Can they play tennis? No, they _____.

🏆 Section 7: Vocabulary & Grammar Check

Unit 9 Review: Abilities & Talents (15 Items)

Question 1: What modal verb is used to express present ability?

💡 Note: 'Can' expresses present ability.

Question 2: What is the negative form of 'can'?

💡 Note: 'Can't' is the negative form.

Question 3: Complete: She can _____ French fluently.

💡 Note: Use base verb after modal 'can'.

Question 4: How do you ask about someone's ability to drive?

💡 Note: Start question with 'Can you...'.

Question 5: Complete: He runs very _____.

💡 Note: 'Fast' is both adjective and adverb.

Question 6: Which adverb means 'spoken smoothly and accurately'?

💡 Note: 'Fluently' means smooth and accurate speech.

Question 7: Complete: Can you swim? Yes, I _____.

💡 Note: Short affirmative answer is 'Yes, I can'.

Question 8: Complete: Can they dance? No, they _____.

💡 Note: Short negative answer is 'No, they can't'.

Question 9: Which adverb means 'with great care and attention'?

💡 Note: Carefully means paying attention.

Question 10: Complete: Please talk _____ because the baby is sleeping.

💡 Note: Quietly means with low sound.

Question 11: Does 'can' change form for he/she/it?

💡 Note: Modal verbs do not take 's'.

Question 12: What is the base verb in 'She can play piano'?

💡 Note: Play is the main base verb.

Question 13: Complete: He can fix computers _____.

💡 Note: Easily is the adverb of manner.

Question 14: Which action involves producing musical vocal sounds?

💡 Note: Singing is vocal music.

Question 15: Complete: I can speak a _____ bit of German.

💡 Note: 'A little bit' expresses small degree.

American Culture: Travel and Holidays

📖 American Culture Notes

Road trips and summer vacations are a major part of American culture. Families visit famous National Parks like Yellowstone and the Grand Canyon, or travel to coastal beaches. People share holiday stories using the past tense ('We visited...', 'The weather was great!').

🗣️ Everyday Dialogue

Karen: How was your vacation in Florida last week, Tom?

Tom: It was wonderful! The weather was warm and sunny every day.

Karen: Did you stay at a hotel near the beach?

Tom: Yes, we stayed at a lovely hotel and visited the national park.

🎥 Video Lesson: Watch VOA Learning English Lesson 10 on YouTube

Free Study Materials: VOA Learning English • Oxford English Resources

📚 Section 1: Vocabulary Boost

Travel & Holidays Vocabulary (20 Items)

Category 1: Travel & Vacation Terms (10 Items)

🛂
Passport
Official document for international travel
"Don't forget your passport at the airport."
🎟️
Plane Ticket
Document giving permission to travel by airplane
"I booked my plane ticket online."
🏨
Hotel
Establishment providing lodging for travelers
"We stayed at a comfortable hotel."
✈️
Flight
A journey made by an airplane
"Our flight to Miami was smooth."
🧳
Suitcase / Luggage
Bags used for carrying personal clothes when traveling
"He packed his clothes in a large suitcase."
🛫
Airport
Complex where commercial aircraft take off and land
"We arrived at the airport early."
📸
Tourist
Person traveling for pleasure and sightseeing
"Many tourists visit the Statue of Liberty."
🏖️
Beach
Sandy shore by the ocean or sea
"We walked along the sunny beach."
⛰️
Mountain
High natural elevation of the Earth's surface
"They hiked up the rocky mountain."
🏙️
Sightseeing
Visiting interesting places as a tourist
"We went sightseeing in New York City."

Category 2: Regular Past Verbs & Time Expressions (10 Items)

🏛️
Visited (-ed)
Went to see a person or place in the past
"We visited Washington D.C. last summer."
🛌
Stayed (-ed)
Lived somewhere temporarily as a guest
"They stayed at a hotel by the sea."
🚗
Traveled (-ed)
Made a journey to another city or country
"She traveled to California last month."
🛬
Arrived (-ed)
Reached a destination at the end of a journey
"The train arrived at 6:00 PM."
📅
Booked (-ed)
Reserved a ticket or room in advance
"I booked a hotel room online."
🚶
Walked (-ed)
Moved on foot in the past
"We walked along the beach at sunset."
😊
Enjoyed (-ed)
Took pleasure or delight in something
"We enjoyed our vacation immensely."
📆
Yesterday
On the day before today
"I arrived home yesterday evening."
🗓️
Last week / year
During the previous week or year
"They visited Florida last year."
⏱️
Two days ago
Two days before the present time
"He booked the flight two days ago."
📝 Section 2: Grammar Focus

Simple Past of To Be (Was/Were) & Regular Past Verbs (-ed)

Simple Past of To Be (Was / Were): I/He/She/It WAS. You/We/They WERE. The hotel was great. The beaches were crowded. It wasn't cold.
Regular Simple Past Verbs (-ed): Add -ed to base verbs for past actions. We visited museum. They stayed at hotel. I booked a ticket.
Past Time Expressions: Use yesterday, last (week/month/year), (time) ago. I traveled to Miami last month. He arrived two hours ago.

Grammar Practice Exercises (15 Items)

1. The weather _____ warm and sunny yesterday.

2. We _____ at a hotel near the beach last week.

3. They _____ the museum two days ago.

4. The flights _____ very expensive last month.

5. I _____ my plane ticket online yesterday.

6. _____ you happy with your holiday hotel?

7. She _____ to California last summer.

8. The train _____ at the station on time.

9. Where _____ you last weekend?

10. We _____ along the beach for two hours.

11. It _____ rain yesterday morning.

12. He _____ his vacation in Mexico.

13. My suitcase _____ very heavy.

14. They arrived two hours _____.

15. Was the hotel clean? Yes, it _____.

🎧 Section 3: Listening Comprehension

Listening: Holiday Travel Reports (15 Items)

Item 1:

Where did Lisa travel last summer?

Item 2:

How was the flight delayed?

Item 3:

Where did they stay?

Item 4:

How long did they walk around the museum?

Item 5:

What did Tom enjoy?

Item 6:

Where was the passport?

Item 7:

When were the flight tickets booked?

Item 8:

Why was the beach crowded?

Item 9:

What time did the bus arrive?

Item 10:

Who did she visit in Oregon?

Item 11:

How was the weather?

Item 12:

How many suitcases did they pack?

Item 13:

How did he travel?

Item 14:

How was the tourist guide?

Item 15:

When did they swim?

🗣️ Section 4: Pronunciation & Oral Drills

Oral Drills: Past Holiday Experiences (15 Drills)

1. "How was your vacation last week?"
💡 Wh-question asking about past events.
2. "It was wonderful! The weather was warm."
💡 Expressing positive past experience.
3. "We stayed at a hotel by the ocean."
💡 Practice past verb 'stayed'.
4. "I traveled to California last summer."
💡 Practice past verb 'traveled'.
5. "We visited famous museums in Washington."
💡 Practice past verb 'visited'.
6. "I booked my plane tickets online two weeks ago."
💡 Practice time phrase 'two weeks ago'.
7. "Were you happy with the hotel service?"
💡 Question format with past of be 'Were you'.
8. "Yes, the hotel staff was very helpful."
💡 Affirmative answer with 'was'.
9. "No, the flight wasn't on time."
💡 Negative past statement with 'wasn't'.
10. "They arrived at the airport yesterday evening."
💡 Practice time phrase 'yesterday evening'.
11. "We walked along the sandy beach at sunset."
💡 Describing past holiday activity.
12. "Did you enjoy your trip to New York?"
💡 Question with 'Did you enjoy...?'
13. "Yes, we enjoyed sightseeing very much!"
💡 Affirmative answer with 'enjoyed'.
14. "Don't forget your passport at home!"
💡 Travel reminder drill.
15. "We had an amazing weekend holiday!"
💡 Concluding past travel phrase.
📖 Section 5: Reading Comprehension

Reading: A Summer Trip to Yellowstone

Last July, the Miller family traveled to Yellowstone National Park for their summer vacation. They drove from Denver in their family car. The journey was long, but the mountain scenery was breathtaking! They stayed at a rustic wooden lodge inside the park. Every morning, they walked along natural trails and visited famous geysers like Old Faithful. The weather was pleasant and sunny. They saw wild bison and eagles! Mr. Miller booked the lodge reservation six months ago. The family enjoyed their vacation so much that they want to return next year!

Comprehension Questions (15 Items)

Question 1: When did the Miller family travel to Yellowstone?

Question 2: How did they travel to the park?

Question 3: Where did they drive from?

Question 4: Where did they stay during the trip?

Question 5: What famous geyser did they visit?

Question 6: How was the weather during their trip?

Question 7: What wild animals did they see?

Question 8: When did Mr. Miller book the lodge reservation?

Question 9: Did the family enjoy their vacation?

Question 10: What do they want to do next year?

Question 11: Was the journey long?

Question 12: What did they do every morning?

Question 13: Was Old Faithful a museum or a geyser?

Question 14: What month was the trip?

Question 15: What kind of scenery did they see?

✏️ Section 6: Grammar & Writing Quiz

Writing & Sentence Structure: Past Tense & Travel (15 Items)

1. Complete: The weather _____ warm yesterday.

2. Fill in: We _____ at a hotel near the beach.

3. Complete: They _____ to California last year.

4. Fill in: I _____ my flight tickets online.

5. Complete: The tourists _____ the museum yesterday.

6. Fill in: The plane _____ at 5:00 PM.

7. Complete: The beaches _____ crowded last summer.

8. Fill in: We walked along the beach two hours _____.

9. Complete: Where _____ you last weekend?

10. Fill in: He _____ his trip to Florida.

11. Complete: It _____ rain yesterday.

12. Fill in: She packed her clothes in a large _____.

13. Complete: Was the hotel clean? Yes, it _____.

14. Fill in: They visited Yellowstone National Park _____ month.

15. Complete: Don't forget your _____ at the airport.

🏆 Section 7: Vocabulary & Grammar Check

Unit 10 Review: Travel & Past Events (15 Items)

Question 1: What official document is required for international travel?

💡 Note: Passport is official travel document.

Question 2: Past form of 'is / am' is:

💡 Note: Singular past of be is 'was'.

Question 3: Past form of 'are' is:

💡 Note: Plural past of be is 'were'.

Question 4: How do you form the regular past tense of 'visit'?

💡 Note: Add -ed for regular past tense.

Question 5: Complete: We _____ at a nice hotel by the beach.

💡 Note: Regular past verb 'stayed'.

Question 6: Which word expresses past time counting backwards (e.g., 'two days ___')?

💡 Note: Ago counts back from present.

Question 7: Complete: I _____ my flight tickets three weeks ago.

💡 Note: Past verb 'booked'.

Question 8: Where do planes take off and land?

💡 Note: Airport handles aircraft traffic.

Question 9: Complete: The weather _____ warm and sunny yesterday.

💡 Note: Weather (uncountable singular) takes 'was'.

Question 10: Complete: The tourists _____ the ancient ruins.

💡 Note: Past action 'visited'.

Question 11: Where do travelers stay on vacation?

💡 Note: Hotels provide lodging.

Question 12: Complete: They arrived two hours _____.

💡 Note: We say 'two hours ago'.

Question 13: What bag is used for carrying clothes during travel?

💡 Note: Suitcase holds travel clothes.

Question 14: Complete: Were you happy with the trip? Yes, I _____.

💡 Note: Subject 'I' takes 'was' in past.

Question 15: Complete: We _____ our vacation in California.

💡 Note: Past verb 'enjoyed'.

American Culture: Staying Fit in America

📖 American Culture Notes

Staying active is very popular in the United States. Many Americans go jogging in the park or exercise at the gym. However, busy lifestyles can cause stress and health issues. When people feel sick, they should rest, drink water, or see a doctor for a prescription.

📚 Vocabulary

Vocabulary Boost

Head
The upper part of the body containing the brain
"I have got a hat on my head."
Shoulder / Arm
Upper body parts for lifting and carrying
"He has a pain in his shoulder."
Leg / Foot
Lower body parts for walking and running
"She broke her leg skiing."
Stomach
The organ where food goes after swallowing
"My stomach hurts today."
Headache
A continuous pain in the head
"I need aspirin for my headache."
Fever
An abnormally high body temperature
"The child has got a high fever."
Medicine
Substance used to treat disease
"You must take your medicine."
🎧 Listening Practice

Listening Comprehension (15 Items)

📝 Audio Transcript:

Listen to the text about Staying Fit in America and practice your comprehension. We use Modal Verb: Should / Shouldn't for Advice to describe everyday activities.

2. Statement 2 about Staying Fit in America is correct. (True/False)

American Culture: Four Seasons in New England

📖 American Culture Notes

The northeast of the United States has four distinct seasons. Fall has beautiful red and gold leaves. Winter is very cold and snowy. Spring brings green trees, rain, and fresh flowers. Summer is hot and sunny, and many families go to the beach to swim and sunbathe.

📚 Vocabulary

Vocabulary Boost

Winter
The coldest season of the year
"It snows a lot in winter."
Spring
The season when flowers start to grow
"It is usually rainy in spring."
Summer
The hottest season of the year
"We go to the beach in summer."
Autumn / Fall
The season when leaves change color
"Fall is my favorite season."
Sunny
Bright with sunlight
"It is a sunny day, let's go out."
Rainy
Having a lot of rain
"Bring an umbrella, it is rainy."
Snowy
Covered with snow
"The mountains are snowy in January."
Windy
With a lot of wind
"It is very windy near the ocean."
🎧 Listening Practice

Listening Comprehension (15 Items)

📝 Audio Transcript:

American Culture: American Road Trips

📖 American Culture Notes

Many Americans love traveling across the country. They have visited national parks like the Grand Canyon or Yosemite. Road trips are a traditional way to see different states, eat local food, and make unforgettable memories with friends and family.

📚 Vocabulary

Vocabulary Boost

Visit
To go to see a place or person
"I have visited many museums."
Travel
To make a journey to a distant place
"She has traveled by train across Italy."
Eat sushi
To eat Japanese raw fish dish
"Have you ever eaten sushi?"
Climb a mountain
To walk up a high mountain
"He has climbed Mount Rainier."
Fly
To travel through the air in an airplane
"We have flown in a small helicopter."
Swim in the ocean
To swim in the sea
"I have swum with dolphins."
🎧 Listening Practice

Listening Comprehension (15 Items)

📝 Audio Transcript:

American Culture: School Rules in the USA

📖 American Culture Notes

American schools have clear rules to keep students safe. Students must arrive on time for classes. They must respect their teachers and classmates. In most public schools, students don't have to wear uniforms, but they must follow a dress code.

📚 Vocabulary

Vocabulary Boost

Must
To be required to do something by rule
"You must wear a seatbelt in the car."
Mustn't
To be prohibited from doing something
"You mustn't smoke in the hospital."
Have to
To be necessary to do due to circumstances
"I have to wash the dishes tonight."
Don't have to
Not necessary, optional
"We don't have to work on Sunday."
Arrive on time
To arrive at the correct scheduled time
"You must arrive on time for school."
Wear a uniform
To wear matching clothing required by school/work
"In some schools, they have to wear uniforms."
🎧 Listening Practice

Listening Comprehension (15 Items)

📝 Audio Transcript:

Listen to the text about School Rules in the USA and practice your comprehension. We use Obligation & Prohibition: Must, Mustn't, Have to, Don't have to to describe everyday activities.

American Culture: City Life vs. Country Life

📖 American Culture Notes

In the United States, lifestyles vary. Some Americans prefer living in big cities like New York because it is faster and more exciting. Others prefer quiet country towns. Country life is usually cheaper, safer, and cleaner, but it can be much slower and less convenient.

📚 Vocabulary

Vocabulary Boost

Bigger / Smaller
Comparative size descriptors
"My room is bigger than yours."
Faster / Slower
Comparative speed descriptors
"Trains are faster than buses."
Cheaper / More expensive
Comparative cost descriptors
"This shirt is cheaper than that jacket."
Cleaner / Dirtier
Comparative cleanliness descriptors
"The country is cleaner than the city."
More exciting
Creating a high level of interest or enthusiasm
"New York is more exciting than Boston."
Better / Worse
Irregular comparative descriptors of quality
"My English is better now."
🎧 Listening Practice

Listening Comprehension (15 Items)

📝 Audio Transcript:

American Culture: Famous American Landmarks

📖 American Culture Notes

The United States is home to some of the world's most famous extremes. Death Valley in California is the hottest and driest place in North America. Alaska is the biggest and coldest state, and Hawaii is the newest and most tropical state.

📚 Vocabulary

Vocabulary Boost

Tallest / Shortest
Extreme height descriptors
"He is the tallest student in class."
Biggest / Smallest
Extreme size descriptors
"Russia is the biggest country."
Hottest / Coldest
Extreme temperature descriptors
"July is the hottest month here."
Most expensive
Highest cost of all
"This is the most expensive restaurant."
Most beautiful
Highest aesthetic value
"She visited the most beautiful lake."
Best / Worst
Irregular superlative quality descriptors
"That was the best day of my life."
🎧 Listening Practice

Listening Comprehension (15 Items)

📝 Audio Transcript:

Listen to the text about Famous American Landmarks and practice your comprehension. We use Superlative Adjectives to describe everyday activities.

American Culture: Smart Cities of Tomorrow

📖 American Culture Notes

Technology is changing our world rapidly. Experts predict that cities in the future will be smart and clean. We will use electric self-driving vehicles, and robots will perform dangerous tasks. We won't use fossil fuels, which will make the air cleaner.

📚 Vocabulary

Vocabulary Boost

Will / Won't
Auxiliaries for future prediction
"I think it will rain tomorrow."
Predict
To say what will happen in the future
"Scientists predict hot summers."
Robot
A machine that can execute complex actions
"Robots will clean our houses."
Self-driving car
A vehicle that drives itself
"We will travel in self-driving cars."
Space travel
Traveling into outer space
"Space travel will be cheap."
Clean energy
Energy that does not pollute
"We will use solar energy in the future."
🎧 Listening Practice

Listening Comprehension (15 Items)

📝 Audio Transcript:

Listen to the text about Smart Cities of Tomorrow and practice your comprehension. We use Future Predictions with Will and Won't to describe everyday activities.

American Culture: Tipping and Restaurant Etiquette

📖 American Culture Notes

In the United States, good service is highly valued. Customers use polite requests like 'could I have' or 'would you mind'. At the end of the meal, it is customary to leave a tip of 15% to 20% of the bill to show appreciation to the waiter.

📚 Vocabulary

Vocabulary Boost

Polite request
Asking for something in a nice way
"Could I have some water, please?"
Bill / Check
The paper showing how much to pay
"Could we have the bill, please?"
Menu
A list of food items and prices
"Could I see the wine menu?"
Can / Could I
Phrases for making polite requests
"Could I borrow your pen?"
Would you like
Phrase for making polite offers
"Would you like some tea?"
Shall I
Phrase for offering help
"Shall I carry your heavy suitcase?"
🎧 Listening Practice

Listening Comprehension (15 Items)

📝 Audio Transcript:

Listen to the text about Tipping and Restaurant Etiquette and practice your comprehension. We use Polite Requests & Offers to describe everyday activities.

American Culture: Mental Wellness in America

📖 American Culture Notes

Talking about emotions is very common in the United States. Many Americans share their feelings openly with friends, family, or counselors. People often practice exercises, jog, or walk in nature because it helps them feel happy and reduces anxiety.

📚 Vocabulary

Vocabulary Boost

Happy
Feeling or showing pleasure or contentment
"She was happy because she won."
Sad
Feeling or showing sorrow; unhappy
"He felt sad when his dog died."
Tired
In need of sleep or rest; weary
"I am tired after the long run."
Nervous / Worried
Anxious or uneasy about something
"She is nervous about the interview."
Excited
Very happy and enthusiastic
"They are excited about the holiday."
Angry
Feeling or showing strong annoyance or hostility
"The teacher was angry with the class."
🎧 Listening Practice

Listening Comprehension (15 Items)

📝 Audio Transcript:

American Culture: Home Cooking in America

📖 American Culture Notes

Many Americans enjoy cooking meals at home. Cooking is a relaxing hobby and a great way to eat fresh, healthy food. Modern American kitchens have many appliances like microwaves, ovens, refrigerators, and blenders to make food preparation quick and easy.

📚 Vocabulary

Vocabulary Boost

Spoon / Fork / Knife
Utensils for eating and cutting
"Eat your soup with a spoon."
Plate / Bowl
Dishes for holding food
"Put the salad in a large bowl."
Pot / Pan
Containers for cooking on the stove
"Fry the onions in a pan."
Oven / Stove
Appliances for baking and heating food
"Bake the cake in the oven."
Refrigerator / Fridge
Appliance for keeping food cold
"The milk is in the fridge."
Microwave
Appliance for heating food quickly
"Warm up your soup in the microwave."
🎧 Listening Practice

Listening Comprehension (15 Items)

📝 Audio Transcript:

Listen to the text about Home Cooking in America and practice your comprehension. We use Quantifiers: Much, Many, A lot of to describe everyday activities.

American Culture: Silicon Valley and Tech Devices

📖 American Culture Notes

Silicon Valley in California is the center of global technology. Major companies build electronic devices and software that we use every day. Smartphones, laptops, and tablets help us connect with the internet, study, work, and chat with friends easily from anywhere.

📚 Vocabulary

Vocabulary Boost

Smartphone
A mobile phone that performs many functions of a computer
"I use my smartphone to take photos."
Laptop / Computer
A portable computer suitable for use while traveling
"He writes emails on his laptop."
Tablet
A flat, portable computer with a touchscreen
"She reads books on her tablet."
Internet
A global computer network
"We use the internet to study."
Social Media
Websites and apps for sharing content and networking
"He spends a lot of time on social media."
Download / Upload
Transferring data to/from the internet
"I want to download a movie."
🎧 Listening Practice

Listening Comprehension (15 Items)

📝 Audio Transcript:
